zh.lifeSummary: 當William Banks於大約1530年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾吉格萊斯維其可時，他的父親Henry Banks21歲，母親Isabella Lister23歲。他於大約1550年與Alice Robinson結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1577年4月在他的家鄉享年48歲。

nameMeanings.name: Banks
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Bank 5, with excrescent -s. The final -s may occasionally represent a plural form, but it is most commonly an arbitrary addition made after the main period of surname formation, perhaps under the influence of patronymic forms with a possessive -s.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Bruacháin ‘descendant of Bruachán’, a byname for a large-bellied person. The English form was chosen because of a mistaken association of the Gaelic name with bruach ‘bank’. Compare Bank 6.

variants.name: Bank
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Mort, Ari, Avraham, Galit, Hyman, Isadore, Mirra, Zvia.

German, Dutch, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German or Middle Low German banc, or Yiddish bank ‘bench, table, counter’, in any of various senses, e.g. a metonymic occupational name for anyone whose work required a bench or counter, for example a butcher, baker, court official, or money changer. The surname of German is also found in Poland and in Czechia.
Danish and Swedish: topographic name from bank ‘(sand)bank’ or a habitational name from a farm named with this word.

variants.name: Bangs
meanings: History: Edward Bangs of Chichester, England, came to Plymouth Colony on board the ‘Anne’ in 1623; he is believed to have been born c. 1592.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When William Banks was born about 1530, in Giggleswick, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry Banks, was 21 and his mother, Isabella Lister, was 23. He married Alice Robinson about 1550. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died in April 1577, in his hometown, at the age of 48.

Il a épousé Alice Robinson environ 1550. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en avril 1577, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 48 ans.
